us-politics.headline: Epstein Case Resurfaces with Suicide Note and Congressional Hearing
us-politics.body:
A purported suicide note from Jeffrey Epstein was released by a judge, reigniting public interest in the high-profile case. Howard Lutnick, a key figure linked to Epstein, testified before a congressional panel amid intense scrutiny.

The hearing drew sharp questions about Lutnick's connections to Epstein. Democratic Representative Ro Khanna suggested former President Trump would have fired Lutnick based on his testimony. The release of the suicide note and Lutnick's appearance have thrust the Epstein scandal back into the national spotlight.

sources.title: Court releases purported suicide note from Jeffrey Epstein
Purported Epstein suicide note released by judge
Secretary Howard Lutnick is questioned by House lawmakers over his Epstein ties
Lutnick testifies before congressional panel investigating Epstein
Khanna: Trump would fire Lutnick if he heard Epstein answers

us-politics.headline: California Political Debates Heat Up with Contentious Primaries
us-politics.body:
California's political landscape is experiencing intense primary battles across multiple races. In the governor's debate, candidates traded sharp criticisms, with the exchange quickly devolving into personal attacks.

Similarly, the Los Angeles mayoral debate saw incumbent Karen Bass sparring with primary challengers. The debates highlighted deep divisions within California's Democratic Party, particularly around issues like immigration and healthcare for undocumented residents.

sources.title: California governor’s debate devolves into bickering as primary nears
Bass spars with primary challengers at LA mayor’s debate
WATCH: California Dems rally around healthcare for illegal immigrants during fiery debate

us-politics.headline: Tensions Simmer in US-Iran Diplomatic Exchanges
us-politics.body:
Diplomatic tensions between the United States and Iran remain high, with an Iranian official dismissing a reported peace proposal as an Americans' wish list. The statement underscores the ongoing challenges in bilateral relations.

Meanwhile, commentators like Sebastian Gorka have used inflammatory language, criticizing war critics in stark terms. The complex geopolitical landscape continues to challenge diplomatic efforts at mutual understanding.

sources.title: Iranian official calls reported peace proposal ‘Americans’ wish list’
Gorka slams Iran war critics as ‘testicularly challenged’
Trump’s pivot toward peace deal

au-politics.headline: Labor Moves to Control Gas Prices and Local Supply
au-politics.body: The federal government will force gas companies to reserve local supply and cap prices on the east coast. New legislation aims to shield Australian consumers from international market volatility and potential shortages. Energy experts warn the policy could have complex economic consequences - with some arguing it will stabilize prices while others suggest it might discourage investment.

sources.title: Gas bills 'no longer hostage' to international market under new laws to drop price
Gas companies will be forced to set aside local supply under major Labor shakeup
Experts warn increasing self-reliance comes at a cost

au-politics.headline: Antisemitism Probe Reveals Deep Tensions in NSW Health System
au-politics.body: A royal commission has heard disturbing testimony about systemic antisemitism within New South Wales health services. Simultaneously, a neo-Nazi activist was charged with inciting hatred after staging a provocative rally at NSW parliament. The incidents highlight ongoing challenges of social cohesion and religious tolerance in Australian public institutions.

sources.title: Australia news live: royal commission hears NSW health system ‘not safe’ for Jewish people; beehives worth $150,000 allegedly stolen from rural property
Neo-Nazi Joel Davis charged with inciting hatred over ‘Abolish the Jewish lobby’ rally at NSW parliament
Jewish hospital patient 'paralysed with fear' after threatening video

au-politics.headline: Victoria's Election Battle Heats Up Over Infrastructure Projects
au-politics.body: The Victorian Coalition has pledged to pause major transmission projects if elected, setting up a significant policy clash with the current government. The promise signals growing tensions over infrastructure development and energy transition strategies. Local communities remain divided on the potential economic and environmental impacts of such large-scale projects.

sources.title: Victorian Coalition vows to pause transmission projects if it wins state election
New gas field in waters near Twelve Apostles given green light
Bushfires now burn at a 'whole different level'. It means rethinking volunteers

world.headline: Iran War Tensions Simmer as Trump Signals Potential Deal
world.body:
Diplomatic efforts to resolve the Iran conflict show fragile signs of progress. Former US President Donald Trump claimed very good talks are possible, suggesting a potential breakthrough in negotiations. Iran is reportedly considering a US proposal while maintaining strategic ambiguity.

Meanwhile, regional tensions remain high. Israel has conducted strikes on Beirut's southern suburbs, and the US has accused Hezbollah of attempting to derail talks. The conflict continues to draw international attention, with China reportedly making unexpected strategic gains.

sources.title: Iran considering US proposal as Trump says war will be 'over quickly'
Iran war live: Trump says deal with Tehran ‘possible’; Israel bombs Beirut
Trump says war will be ‘over quickly’ as Iran reviews US peace proposal
US says Hezbollah is ‘trying to derail talks’ with Israel

world.headline: Global Conflict and Diplomatic Tensions Escalate Across Multiple Fronts
world.body:
Geopolitical tensions are rising in multiple global hotspots. Russia has instructed diplomats to leave Kyiv, hinting at potential massive strikes, while North Korea declared it will not be bound by nuclear non-proliferation treaties.

In the Middle East, Israeli strikes have killed five Palestinians in Gaza, and US lawmakers are pushing for transparency about Israel's nuclear capabilities. The international community watches these developments with growing concern about potential escalations.

sources.title: Russia tells diplomats to leave Kyiv in case Moscow launches mass strikes
North Korea says it is not bound by any treaty on nuclear non-proliferation
Israeli strikes kill five Palestinians across Gaza in one day
In rare push, US lawmakers demand transparency on Israel nuclear capability

world.headline: France Launches One-Euro Student Meals in Social Welfare Push
world.body:
France has introduced a groundbreaking policy of one-euro canteen meals for all students, marking a significant step in social welfare. The initiative aims to ensure affordable nutrition for students across the country.

The program reflects a broader commitment to addressing economic challenges and supporting youth. By making school meals accessible and affordable, France is investing in the well-being of its younger generation.

sources.title: France introduces one‑euro canteen meals for all students
South Korean court reduces Han Duck-soo’s prison term in martial law case

ai.headline: OpenAI Drama Escalates: Musk's Legal Battle Reveals Internal Tensions
ai.body:
The ongoing legal battle between Elon Musk and OpenAI has exposed deep fractures within the AI startup's leadership. Testimony from key executives like Mira Murati suggests a breakdown of trust, with Murati reportedly stating she could not rely on Sam Altman's words. Musk's desperate attempts to recruit Altman to Tesla and control the narrative have only intensified the conflict.

The trial has become a high-stakes drama of Silicon Valley ambition - revealing how personal relationships and competing visions can unravel even the most promising technological partnerships. Musk's loyalists are now becoming potential liabilities in a case that could reshape the AI industry's future.

sources.title: Musk’s biggest loyalist became his biggest liability
Elon Musk’s Last-Ditch Effort to Control OpenAI: Recruit Sam Altman to Tesla
Barry Diller trusts Sam Altman. But ‘trust is irrelevant’ as AGI nears, he says.
How Elon Musk left OpenAI, according to Greg Brockman
Mira Murati tells the court that she couldn’t trust Sam Altman’s words

ai.headline: AI and Space Converge: Anthropic-SpaceX Deal Signals New Tech Frontier
ai.body:
Anthropic has struck a landmark deal with SpaceX, expanding Claude's code usage limits and signaling a deeper convergence between AI and space technology. The partnership comes as TSMC struggles to meet surging AI chip demand - tapping wind power to support energy-intensive computing needs.

The collaboration hints at a broader trend: tech giants are increasingly looking beyond traditional boundaries. SpaceX is even exploring a massive $119 billion chip factory in Texas, underscoring how space and AI technologies are becoming deeply intertwined.

sources.title: Anthropic raises Claude Code usage limits, credits new deal with SpaceX
TSMC taps wind power as AI chip demand soars, Taiwan feels energy crunch
Anthropic Gets in Bed With SpaceX as the AI Race Turns Weird
SpaceX may spend up to $119B on ‘Terafab’ chip factory in Texas

btc.headline: Bitcoin Faces Quantum Computing Threat by 2030
btc.body: Bitcoin's cryptographic foundations could crumble within the next decade as quantum computing advances. Experts warn that the network must proactively develop post-quantum migration strategies to prevent potential security breaches. Project Eleven's CEO emphasized that this transition will be more complex than previous upgrades like Taproot - requiring immediate and strategic technical intervention.

sources.title: Bitcoin’s post-quantum migration will be harder than Taproot and needs to start now, Project Eleven CEO says
Bitcoin, Ethereum 'Q-Day' Quantum Threat Could Arrive as Soon as 2030: Report

btc.headline: VanEck Predicts Bitcoin Could Hit $1 Million by 2031
btc.body: Investment firm VanEck sees massive potential for Bitcoin, projecting the cryptocurrency could reach $1 million within five years. The firm drew parallels between Bitcoin's adoption trajectory and the evolution of video game markets - suggesting a transformative growth path. This bullish forecast comes amid increasing institutional interest and growing mainstream acceptance of digital assets.

sources.title: VanEck says Bitcoin could hit $1 million in five years, likens adoption to video games
Spot Bitcoin ETFs solved access, but custody, advisors and plumbing still lag, panelists say
Grant Cardone says bitcoin-real estate strategy could outperform REITs, adds more BTC to treasury

btc.headline: U.S. Bitcoin Reserve Update Imminent - White House Signals Movement
btc.body: The White House is preparing to release details about the U.S. Bitcoin Reserve in the coming weeks. This development signals continued government engagement with cryptocurrency infrastructure. Meanwhile, Eric Trump's American Bitcoin reported an $82 million quarterly loss - highlighting the volatile landscape of Bitcoin-related enterprises.

sources.title: U.S. Bitcoin Reserve update coming in 'next few weeks," White House adviser says
Eric Trump’s American Bitcoin reports $82 million loss in Q1 amid ‘record’ mining production
Eric Trump takes shot at JPMorgan rethinking bitcoin after 'crapping' on asset

food.name: Miso Butter Corn Risotto
food.description: Creamy risotto with sweet corn, umami miso, and a hint of smoky butter
food.tip: Toast the rice in butter before adding liquid to enhance nutty flavors

health.name: Desk Spine Reset
health.description: A 3-minute micro-routine to decompress and realign your spine after prolonged sitting
health.tip: Use a foam roller vertically along your spine, rolling slowly from tailbone to neck
